message:
fix: use round() instead of floor() for consistent alignment padding

Change padding calculations from floor() to round() in place(),
Style::apply_padding_height(), and Style::apply_padding_width() to
match the behavior of join_horizontal/join_vertical.

The inconsistency (floor vs round) caused visual misalignment when:
- Content is placed within a container using place()
- Style alignment is applied with fractional padding

Affected locations:
- lib.rs:place() - horizontal left_pad, vertical top_pad, line_left
- style.rs:apply_padding_height() - top_extra
- style.rs:apply_padding_width() - left_pad

All changes reference bd-3vqi which tracks this alignment inconsistency.

Example: A 9-char wide cell with Position::Center (factor 0.5) and
3 chars content should have 3 chars on each side. With floor():
(6 * 0.5) = 3.0 → 3 left, 3 right ✓
But (7 * 0.5) = 3.5 → 3 left, 4 right ✗ (asymmetric)
With round(): 3.5 → 4 left, 3 right (closer to centered)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
